bits.premise: Avoiding autism testing keeps the diagnosis from becoming real.

lines.text: -What's up?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I'm trying to talk to more women now,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: but it should be good 'cause I don't have autism
lines.label: setup

lines.text: 'cause I've never been tested.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: And I think that's how you beat it.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: I think that's the only cure I've heard of.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: But my brother, he got tested, right?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So they got him.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: And you can't give it back.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: People always, they're like, "Does he have it full on?"
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I'm like, "Well, it's not part time.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: "It's not weekends hit the club Monday, back to puzzles."
lines.label: tag

lines.text: I did try to get tested too when I was in Canada,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: where it's free there, but they're busy.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So they, you know, they phoned me back, they're like,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: they're like, "Sorry sir, the wait time is seven years."
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I'm like, "What the f--?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: "The final divide, autism seven years?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: "No, that's 3,461 days, no.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: "Oh, I can't do that, that's a Tuesday.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: "No, Tuesday's when the train comes."
lines.label: tag

lines.text: That's why I said, thank you guys.
lines.label: fluff
